class.name: FmodAudioEffectReverb
class.brief_description: A reverb effect for adding spatial ambience and simulated acoustic spaces to audio.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectReverb creates the impression of sound being played in a physical space by simulating reflections and decay characteristics of various environments. Use this effect to add depth, warmth, and realism to audio sources. Adjust the dry/wet mix to control the balance between the original signal and the reverberated signal. Increase room size for cathedral-like spaces, or decrease for small room ambience.

members.member: Controls the absorption of high frequencies in the virtual room. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values simulate softer surfaces (carpets, curtains) that absorb more high frequencies, creating a warmer, darker reverb tail.
The level of the original (dry) signal. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. A value of 1.0 passes the original signal at full volume. Lower values reduce the direct sound, emphasizing the reverb effect.
High-pass filter applied to the reverb input. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values remove more low frequencies from the reverb, preventing muddiness in the reverberated signal.
The amount of feedback in the predelay section. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Controls how much of the predelay output is fed back into itself, affecting the density of early reflections.
The delay time before the reverb tail begins, measured in milliseconds. Range: 0.0 to 300.0 ms. Simulates the time it takes for sound to reach the first reflecting surfaces in a room.

The size of the simulated acoustic space. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values create the impression of larger spaces (halls, cathedrals) with longer decay times, while lower values simulate smaller rooms.
The stereo spread of the reverb. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values create a wider stereo image in the reverberated signal, simulating reflections from wider spaces.
The level of the reverberated (wet) signal. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Controls how much of the processed reverb signal is mixed with the dry signal. A value of 0.0 produces no reverb effect.
